book_metadata.title: Option Spread Strategies: Trading Up, Down, and Sideways Markets
book_metadata.author: Anthony J. Saliba with Joseph C. Corona and Karen E. Johnson
book_metadata.focus: Options spread trading strategies for directional, neutral, and volatility plays

strategy_selection_process: 1. Determine market forecast (bullish, bearish, neutral, breakout)
2. Assess magnitude of expected move (moderate vs large)
4. Evaluate current implied volatility levels and term structure
5. Assess risk appetite (limited vs unlimited risk tolerance)
6. Select strategy that matches all parameters
7. Use box/jelly roll pricing to choose calls vs puts at execution
author_framework.risk_management_philosophy: If you are wrong, get out. No hoping or praying. Have exit plan before entry.
key_principles: There is no free lunch - every upside has a corresponding downside
Long gamma comes at the cost of negative theta (time decay)
Short gamma earns positive theta but has adverse delta movement
Use synthetically equivalent positions for better exit pricing
Scale into and out of positions for psychological comfort
Support and resistance levels serve dual purpose: strike selection AND risk management
Rolling positions via vertical spreads or butterflies maintains exposure while reducing risk

iv_framework.iv_skew_usage: Sell high-IV strikes, buy low-IV strikes when skew is steep
iv_framework.term_structure_usage: Compare front-month vs back-month IV for calendar spread direction

strategies.name: Covered Write (Buy-Write)

entry_rules.market_forecast: Neutral to moderately bullish on underlying
entry_rules.iv_conditions: Higher IV preferred for larger premiums and higher returns

strike_selection.neutral_outlook: Sell ATM call for maximum time decay
strike_selection.mildly_bullish: Sell OTM call at expected target price
strike_selection.moderately_bullish: Sell further OTM call for more upside participation

entry_rules.dte: 45 days or less for income generation; longer for downside protection
entry_rules.avoid_when: Bearish or strongly bullish outlook; rate of return should not drive selection

exit_rules.profit_target: Return to call (stock at or above strike at expiration)
exit_rules.max_loss: Breakeven = stock price - premium received; exit if broken
exit_rules.time_exit: At or before expiration; must manage or roll
exit_rules.view_change: Liquidate immediately if view shifts bearish or rampantly bullish

adjustments.stock_rises_through_strike: Roll up via long call spread or diagonal up-and-out
adjustments.stock_weakens: Roll down via short call spread for additional protection
adjustments.emergency_exit: Buy put of same strike/expiry to create conversion (neutral)

risk_management.max_risk: Substantial downside risk minus premium received
risk_management.max_reward: Premium received plus stock appreciation to strike
risk_management.breakeven: Stock purchase price minus premium received

greeks_profile.delta: Positive (stock delta 100 minus short call delta)

strategies.notes: Synthetic equivalent of short put. NOT a fire-and-forget strategy. Requires attention and multiple adjustments. Late-night TV claims of easy money are misleading.

strategies.name: Bull Call Spread (Debit Call Spread)

entry_rules.market_forecast: Moderately bullish; expecting move to short strike
entry_rules.iv_conditions: Good when IV is high (reduces cost vs outright long call)
entry_rules.strike_selection: Long strike near current price; short strike at price target
entry_rules.dte: Expiration matching time horizon of forecast move
entry_rules.magnitude: Expected moderate upward move equal to strike differential

exit_rules.profit_target: Maximum profit at or above short strike at expiration
exit_rules.max_loss: Limited to debit paid for spread
exit_rules.early_exit: If forecast changes, liquidate or use synthetic equivalent
exit_rules.roll: Sell overlapping butterfly to roll to higher strikes

greeks_profile.gamma: Positive near lower strike, negative near upper strike
greeks_profile.theta: Negative near lower strike (hurts), positive near upper strike (helps)
greeks_profile.vega: Positive near lower strike, negative near upper strike

strategies.notes: Synthetically equivalent to bull put spread. Use box pricing to determine whether call or put spread offers better value at execution.

strategies.name: Bull Put Spread (Credit Put Spread)

entry_rules.market_forecast: Moderately bullish; expect price to stay above short put strike
entry_rules.strike_selection: Short put at support level; long put below for protection
entry_rules.dte: Shorter duration for faster theta decay
entry_rules.income_generation: Sell OTM put spreads below strong support for premium collection

exit_rules.profit_target: Both puts expire worthless; keep full credit
exit_rules.time_exit: Positive theta; time works in favor if price stays above short strike

strategies.notes: Synthetically equivalent to bull call spread. Credit spread version of bullish vertical. Good for income generation with defined risk.

strategies.name: Bear Call Spread (Credit Call Spread)

entry_rules.market_forecast: Moderately bearish; expect price to stay below short call strike
entry_rules.strike_selection: Short call at resistance; long call above for protection
entry_rules.dte: Shorter duration for faster theta decay

exit_rules.profit_target: Both calls expire worthless; keep full credit

strategies.notes: Synthetically equivalent to bear put spread. Good for selling premium above resistance with capped risk.

strategies.name: Bear Put Spread (Debit Put Spread)

entry_rules.market_forecast: Moderately bearish; expecting decline to short strike
entry_rules.iv_conditions: Good when IV is high (reduces cost vs outright long put)
entry_rules.strike_selection: Long put near current price; short put at downside target
entry_rules.dte: Match expiration to expected time horizon

exit_rules.profit_target: Maximum at or below short strike at expiration
exit_rules.roll: Sell overlapping butterfly to roll to lower strikes

greeks_profile.gamma: Positive near long strike, negative near short strike
greeks_profile.theta: Negative near long strike, positive near short strike
greeks_profile.vega: Positive near long strike, negative near short strike

strategies.notes: Good for countertrend trades and limited-risk bearish positions.

strategies.name: Collar (Bearish)

entry_rules.market_forecast: Sharp bearish move expected below put strike
entry_rules.trigger_price: Place long put at anticipated breakdown trigger level
entry_rules.resistance: Place short call at significant overhead resistance
entry_rules.iv_conditions: Good when IV is moderate-to-high (offsetting vega minimizes IV risk)
entry_rules.iv_skew: Flat skew makes collar more attractive (call IV >= put IV)
entry_rules.timing: Imprecise timing OK due to theta/vega offset structure

exit_rules.successful: Roll long put down via selling vertical put spreads to lock in profits
exit_rules.unsuccessful: Stop-loss on short call if resistance broken; buy back short call
exit_rules.no_trigger: Roll entire collar to next expiry via calendar spreads

risk_management.max_risk: Unlimited above short call strike (must use stop-loss)

greeks_profile.gamma: Near zero between strikes; positive near put, negative near call

strategies.notes: Combined with long stock creates synthetic bull vertical spread. Choose over outright puts when timing is uncertain. Naked short call risk requires stop-loss discipline.

strategies.name: Reverse Collar (Bullish)

entry_rules.market_forecast: Sharp bullish move expected above call strike
entry_rules.trigger_price: Place long call near breakout trigger level
entry_rules.support: Place short put at significant underlying support
entry_rules.iv_conditions: Steep IV skew (OTM puts at higher IV than OTM calls) makes this especially attractive

exit_rules.successful: Roll long call up via selling vertical call spreads
exit_rules.unsuccessful: Stop-loss on short put if support broken
exit_rules.no_trigger: Roll to next expiry via calendar spreads

strategies.notes: Combined with short stock creates synthetic bear vertical spread. Exploits IV skew by selling expensive OTM puts and buying cheaper OTM calls.

strategies.name: Long Straddle

entry_rules.market_forecast: Large magnitude breakout expected in either direction
entry_rules.catalysts: Earnings, government reports, corporate events, macro events, technical breakouts
entry_rules.iv_conditions: Best entered when IV is LOW (cheaper premiums)
entry_rules.timing: Must be fairly precise; clock is the enemy

exit_rules.profit_target: Roll profitable leg via selling vertical spreads
exit_rules.max_loss: Total debit paid (premium of both options)
exit_rules.time_stop: Set date beyond which you will not hold position
exit_rules.price_stop: Set price below which you will not hold position
exit_rules.exit_on_lesser: Exit when EITHER time or price stop is hit first

risk_management.max_reward: Unlimited upside; substantial downside to zero
risk_management.breakevens: Strike minus debit (downside); strike plus debit (upside)

greeks_profile.delta: Near zero at ATM; becomes positive as underlying rises, negative as it falls
greeks_profile.theta: NEGATIVE (time decay is the major cost)

strategies.notes: Seductive due to unlimited profit potential. Traders often overlook theta/vega costs. Death by a thousand cuts from daily time decay. Must have both correct direction AND timing.

strategies.name: Long Strangle

entry_rules.strike_selection: OTM strikes approximately equidistant from current price for delta neutrality

exit_rules.same_as_straddle: Time stop + price stop; exit on lesser
exit_rules.roll_profitable_leg: Sell vertical spreads to capture gains

risk_management.breakevens: Put strike minus debit; call strike plus debit

greeks_profile.delta: Near zero; double-peaked gamma profile near expiry

strategies.notes: Less aggressive than straddle. Wider breakeven points but cheaper cost. Near expiry, delta is ~zero between strikes.

strategies.name: Short Straddle

entry_rules.iv_conditions: Best entered when IV is HIGH (collect larger premium)
entry_rules.catalysts: Post-volatile period rest, seasonal quiet, holiday periods
entry_rules.timing: After volatile period when market needs to consolidate

exit_rules.profit_target: Time decay erodes position value; collect theta daily
exit_rules.stop_loss_price: Set price stop on straddle value for rising IV scenario
exit_rules.stop_loss_underlying: Key price levels that indicate breakout
exit_rules.immediate_action: If breakout occurs, manage short option actively

risk_management.breakevens: Strike minus credit; strike plus credit

greeks_profile.delta: Near zero at strike; moves against trader

strategies.notes: UNLIMITED RISK. Must use stop-losses. Theta income can vanish in one large move. Set stop on both underlying price and straddle price. Not suitable for most retail traders.

strategies.name: Short Strangle

entry_rules.iv_conditions: Higher IV preferred for larger credit
entry_rules.strike_selection: OTM strikes at support/resistance levels equidistant from price

exit_rules.same_as_short_straddle: Price and straddle-value stop losses required

risk_management.breakevens: Put strike minus credit; call strike plus credit

strategies.notes: Less aggressive than short straddle but still UNLIMITED RISK. Wider profit zone but smaller max profit.

strategies.name: Long Call Butterfly

entry_rules.market_forecast: Directionless; underlying hovering near middle strike

strike_selection.middle_strike: At mean-reversion price of trading range
strike_selection.wings: At support and resistance levels of trading range

entry_rules.iv_conditions: Best when IV is high (short middle strike benefits from IV decline)
entry_rules.dte: Shorter-dated bears fruit faster but costs more; blend expirations for longer ranges
entry_rules.identification: Use technical analysis to find support/resistance/mean-reversion levels

exit_rules.take_partial_profits: Scale out 2-3 at a time from 10-lot
exit_rules.synthetic_exit: Buy back straddle at middle strike to leave long strangle
exit_rules.avoid_greed: Don't hold for 'one more day' of theta; breakout can wipe accumulated profits

adjustments.range_wider_than_expected: Add butterfly at adjacent strikes to create condor
adjustments.range_narrows: Sell embedded butterfly to tighten position

risk_management.breakevens: Lower strike + debit; upper strike - debit

greeks_profile.delta: Near zero at middle strike; positive below, negative above
greeks_profile.gamma: Negative at middle strike; positive at wings
greeks_profile.theta: POSITIVE at middle strike (earns time decay)
greeks_profile.vega: Negative at middle strike (benefits from falling IV)

strategies.notes: Limited-risk version of short straddle. Synthetic: short straddle wrapped by long strangle. Call, put, and iron butterflies are synthetically equivalent.

strategies.name: Long Iron Butterfly
strategies.notes: Iron version uses mixed puts and calls. Credit trade. Same Greeks as call/put butterfly.

strategies.name: Long Call Condor

entry_rules.market_forecast: Directionless; wider trading range than butterfly can cover

strike_selection.inner_strikes: Spanning the mean-reversion zone of the trading range

entry_rules.vs_butterfly: Use when range is too wide for butterfly or mean-reversion is a zone not a point

exit_rules.same_as_butterfly: Scale out, watch for breakouts, exit on support/resistance breach

adjustments.range_narrows: Sell embedded butterfly from condor to tighten into butterfly

risk_management.max_reward: Inner strike differential minus debit (less than butterfly)

strategies.notes: Wider profit zone than butterfly but costs more and has lower max profit. Call, put, and iron condors are synthetically equivalent.

strategies.name: Long Iron Condor
strategies.notes: Most popular version for retail traders. Credit trade with defined risk. Collect premium if price stays between inner strikes.

strategies.name: Long Calendar Spread (Horizontal Time Spread)

sideways_use.strike: ATM (at current price) for neutral trade
sideways_use.iv_conditions: Best when IV is LOW; benefits from rising IV (long vega)

directional_use.bullish: Strike above current price (target for upward move)
directional_use.bearish: Strike below current price (target for downward move)
directional_use.timing: Short-dated option expires at expected arrival time

term_structure.favorable: Short-dated IV higher than long-dated IV (sell expensive, buy cheap)
term_structure.unfavorable: Short-dated IV lower than long-dated (avoid or use short calendar)

exit_rules.profit_target: Spread reaches maximum value at strike at short-option expiry
exit_rules.early_arrival: If price hits target early, assess whether to hold or roll to new strike
exit_rules.at_expiration: MUST close or roll before short option expires; otherwise creates naked directional

adjustments.roll_strikes: Two vertical spreads in two separate months to shift target
adjustments.roll_time: Buy back short month, sell next month (if forecast still valid)

risk_management.max_reward: Cannot be precisely determined (depends on IV at short expiry)
risk_management.key_risk: Early exercise of short deep-ITM leg

greeks_profile.delta: Positive below strike, negative above (gravitates toward strike)
greeks_profile.theta: POSITIVE at strike (earns time decay differentially)
greeks_profile.vega: POSITIVE (benefits from rising IV; long-dated has more vega)

strategies.notes: Call and put calendars are synthetically equivalent (linked by jelly roll). Unique: positive theta AND positive vega simultaneously. Great when IV is low and market is sideways.

strategies.name: Short Calendar Spread

entry_rules.market_forecast: Sharp movement away from strike in either direction
entry_rules.iv_conditions: Best when IV is HIGH (sell expensive long-dated, buy cheap short-dated)
entry_rules.term_structure: Favorable when short-dated IV is lower (upward sloping term structure)
entry_rules.advantage_over_straddle: Short vega; benefits from IV decline after breakout

exit_rules.failed_breakout: Get out - forecast is wrong, time decay works against you

risk_management.max_risk: Difference in option prices at short expiry (unquantifiable at entry)

greeks_profile.delta: Negative below strike, positive above (moves favorably away from strike)

strategies.notes: Breakout play with defined but unquantifiable risk. Better than long straddle when IV is high and expected to fall post-event.

strategies.name: Call Ratio Spread

entry_rules.market_forecast: Slow, limited upward move toward short strike with declining IV
best_conditions: Volatility skew: buy low-IV strike, sell high-IV strike

strike_selection.short_strike: At target price / resistance level / congestion zone

entry_rules.iv_conditions: High IV preferred (selling extra premium); expect IV to decline

exit_rules.market_behavior_changes: Exit if underlying accelerates beyond forecast
exit_rules.discipline: Iron fist risk management - ratio spread punishes the greedy

adjustments.cap_risk: Buy additional call at higher strike to convert to butterfly
adjustments.roll_shorts_up: Buy back short calls, sell higher strikes (1:1 or ratioed)
adjustments.adverse_move_down: If done for credit, position may still profit; can hold for rebound

risk_management.max_risk: UNLIMITED to the upside above short strike
risk_management.downside_risk: Limited to debit if done for debit; zero if done for credit

greeks_profile.delta: Positive (grows more positive with mild upward move and time)
greeks_profile.gamma: Negative near short strike; positive near long strike

strategies.notes: UNLIMITED RISK on one side. Essentially a one-winged butterfly. Can be viewed as bull call spread + extra short call. NOT for traders who cannot handle overnight risk. Convert to butterfly to cap risk if nervous.

strategies.name: Put Ratio Spread

entry_rules.market_forecast: Slow, limited downward move toward short strike with declining IV

strike_selection.short_strike: At target price / support level / congestion zone

adjustments.cap_risk: Buy additional put at lower strike to convert to butterfly
adjustments.roll_shorts_down: Buy back short puts, sell lower strikes

risk_management.max_risk: Substantial to the downside below short strike (to zero)

greeks_profile.delta: Negative (grows more negative with mild down move and time)

strategies.notes: Mirror image of call ratio spread. Use for contratrend corrections. Convert to butterfly to manage risk.

strategies.name: Call Backspread

entry_rules.catalysts: Bullish news, earnings, takeovers, technical breakouts from consolidation
entry_rules.iv_conditions: Enter when IV is LOW; benefits from rising IV (long vega)
entry_rules.timing: CRITICAL - timing must be precise; wrong timing causes losses even with correct direction

exit_rules.timing_wrong: If breakout doesn't materialize quickly, time decay erodes position
exit_rules.between_strikes_danger: Position can go negative delta between strikes near expiry
exit_rules.successful: Roll long calls up via selling vertical spreads

risk_management.max_risk: (K2-K1) + debit or - credit. Maximum at short strike at expiry
risk_management.downside_risk: Limited (possibly credit if done for credit)

greeks_profile.delta: Starts near zero; becomes increasingly positive on rally

strategies.notes: Exact opposite of ratio spread. Leveraged directional play. Timing is everything - passage of time or falling IV can make position go negative delta BETWEEN strikes even if direction is correct. Danger zone: price stuck between strikes near expiry.

strategies.name: Put Backspread

entry_rules.catalysts: Bearish news, poor earnings, scandals, technical breakdowns

exit_rules.same_as_call_backspread: Time is enemy; exit if breakout fails to materialize

risk_management.max_reward: Substantial to downside (to zero); limited to credit on upside

greeks_profile.delta: Starts near zero; becomes increasingly negative on selloff

strategies.notes: Mirror image of call backspread. Best for crash/panic scenarios. VIX spikes benefit this position via positive vega.

strategies.name: Box Spread (Arbitrage/Pricing Tool)

legs.description: Bull call spread + bear put spread (same strikes, same expiry)
OR bear call spread + bull put spread (same strikes, same expiry)

entry_rules.usage: Pricing tool to determine whether call or put vertical offers better value

rules.bull_spread: If call ask + put bid > box: sell put spread. If < box: buy call spread
rules.bear_spread: If call bid + put ask > box: sell call spread. If < box: buy put spread

risk_management.description: No delta, gamma, theta, or vega. Pure interest rate component.

strategies.notes: Not a trading strategy per se but a critical tool for choosing between synthetically equivalent spreads. At expiration, box value always equals strike differential.

common_adjustment_techniques.rolling_verticals: Sell vertical spread in profitable direction to lock in gains while maintaining exposure
common_adjustment_techniques.rolling_to_butterfly: Sell butterfly that overlaps current vertical to shift strikes up or down
common_adjustment_techniques.capping_ratio_spread: Buy additional wing option to convert ratio spread to limited-risk butterfly
common_adjustment_techniques.calendar_rolling: Buy calendar to roll long option, sell calendar to roll short option to next month
common_adjustment_techniques.synthetic_exit: Use synthetically equivalent position for better pricing on exit (especially when ITM options have wide spreads)

key_warnings_from_author: There is no free lunch - every strategy has a corresponding risk
Long gamma always costs theta; short theta always has adverse gamma
Rate of return should NEVER be the deciding factor for covered writes
Short straddles/strangles are addictive but have unlimited risk
Long straddles/strangles look great on paper but time decay death is real
Ratio spreads punish the greedy - have an iron fist risk management plan
Backspreads can go negative delta between strikes if timing is wrong
Stop-loss orders do not protect when the market is closed (gap risk)
If your forecast is wrong, GET OUT. No hoping, no praying.
Covered writes are NOT fire-and-forget strategies despite TV advertising
